# RSS-BOOKSTORE

Canonical repository: https://github.com/file-bricks/RSS-BOOKSTORE
Sibling project: https://github.com/file-bricks/RSS-BOOK
Owner: file-bricks
Status: alpha, GitHub/sideloading distribution
License: MIT

RSS-BOOKSTORE is a local-first Manifest V3 Chromium extension for power users
who want RSS and Atom feed entries saved as browser bookmarks and mirrored into
a Windows folder as `.url` shortcut files.

## Primary Use

- Add RSS/Atom feeds in the browser extension options page.
- Store feed entries as Chromium bookmarks.
- Optionally mirror entries to a local Windows folder through a Python Native
  Messaging host.
- Use `SYNC` mode for bidirectional bookmark/folder synchronization.
- Prefer RSS-BOOK when a store-friendly extension without Native Messaging is
  needed.

## Important Files

- `README.md` - install, Native Messaging setup, usage and troubleshooting.
- `manifest.json` - Manifest V3 extension metadata.
- `sw.js` - service worker and feed update flow.
- `lib/` - bookmarks, permissions, Native Messaging and sync helpers.
- `ui/` - popup and options UI.
- `native_host/` - Python Native Messaging host and PowerShell installer.
- `scripts/package_github_release.ps1` - GitHub release ZIP builder.
- `tests/` - JavaScript extension tests and Python Native Messaging tests.

## Search Phrases

- RSS-BOOKSTORE file-bricks
- RSS Atom browser extension bookmarks Native Messaging
- Windows RSS folder sync .url files browser extension
- local-first RSS bookmark extension Chromium
- RSS-BOOK vs RSS-BOOKSTORE
- Native Messaging RSS bookmarks extension

## Disambiguation

RSS-BOOKSTORE is not a hosted feed reader, not a cloud RSS service, not a
general bookmark manager, not RSS-Bridge, not FreshRSS, not Miniflux, and not
the Chrome Web Store build of RSS-BOOK. RSS-BOOKSTORE is the sideloaded
power-user companion with a local Windows folder mirror.

## Safety Boundary

The extension requests per-feed host permissions instead of blanket access.
Folder sync depends on a local Native Messaging host registered by the user.
The project is alpha software and should be validated before everyday use.
